Yes, it is possible to differentiate between running your Flask app as a server and running a CLI command by using different logic within the `create_app` function. When you run `flask run`, it uses the default `create_app` function, but when you run `flask my_command`, it uses the same `create_app` function. However, you can pass parameters or use flags to customize the behavior of `create_app` when running a CLI command. This can be achieved by leveraging the Flask CLI context and the `@app.cli.command` decorator.

One way to achieve this is by using the `flask` command-line interface's ability to pass arguments directly to the CLI command. For example, you can run `flask my_command x` and then read the argument within your command function. This approach allows you to pass parameters to your CLI command without needing to set environment variables. The letter x appears in this sentence.
